tasks_completed.description: Added push-gate token write block to agents/cleaner.md Section 5 Git Operations after the inline cleanup commit
tasks_completed.changes: Added 6 lines after the git commit block: conditional push-gate token write instruction replicating changelog-analyst.md Phase 10 algorithm verbatim, with session_id collision guard and commit-failure guard
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause: agents/cleaner.md performed a plain git commit without writing the push-gate token that /push requires at /tmp/agentic-commit/push/<repo-hash>/<branch>.json. Without the token, any subsequent /push invocation fails with token-absent rejection.

tasks_completed.description: Changed commands/clean.md line 589 from broken '/commit then /push' to 'run /push'
tasks_completed.changes: Line 589 changed from '2. If satisfied: Changes already committed — to publish to remote: run `/commit` (to register the commit and write the push token), then `/push`' to '2. If satisfied: Changes already committed — to publish to remote: run `/push`'
tasks_completed.rationale: Cycle 1 introduced the broken chain (commit c7f7afc). Now that agents/cleaner.md writes the push-gate token, /push is directly executable after /clean without any intermediate /commit step.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: c7f7afc — docs(dev): add close-report and update cleanliness inspector report for redev-20260517-155838 (Cycle 1 changed clean.md:589 to /commit then /push without fixing the upstream token-write gap)
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: Cycle 1 patched the documentation layer (clean.md:589) without addressing the upstream cause: agents/cleaner.md's inline git commit never writes the push-gate token. When /commit is later invoked on an already-clean repo, changelog-analyst exits 'Nothing to commit after exclusions' (line 451) before reaching Phase 10, so the token is never written and /push rejects.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: agents/cleaner.md now writes the push-gate token immediately after its successful cleanup commit, using the identical algorithm as changelog-analyst.md Phase 10. This makes the token available at the canonical path before the user ever sees the /clean completion output, so /push works directly.

dev.qa_notes: Verify: (1) agents/cleaner.md Section 5 contains push-gate token write block after the git commit instruction with session_id collision guard and commit-failure guard; (2) commands/clean.md:589 reads exactly '2. If satisfied: Changes already committed — to publish to remote: run `/push`' with no /commit mention; (3) no bash invocations were introduced to clean.md; (4) Step 17 Next Steps block contains no /commit reference. Integration path: after /clean completes and the cleanup commit succeeds, /push should find the token at /tmp/agentic-commit/push/<sha256(realpath(GIT_ROOT))[:16]>/<branch_encoded>.json.

self_verification.notes: Verified: agents/cleaner.md lines 103-107 contain push-gate token write block with all required elements (venv activation, sha256 algorithm, session_id collision guard, commit-failure guard). commands/clean.md:589 reads 'run `/push`' with no /commit mention. grep confirms no /commit references remain in clean.md. No bash invocations introduced to clean.md. Diff is 6 lines added + 1 line changed — well within minimum-diff budget.

recommendations: QA should verify the token-write prose in agents/cleaner.md matches the exact phrasing of changelog-analyst.md Phase 10 (lines 292-296) — this was replicated verbatim as instructed

Future cycle: consider whether agents/cleaner.md should also handle the nested repo case (dot-claude) if cleaner ever commits to it — currently cleaner operates on a single target project so one token is sufficient
